hazards.description: Wrong PID delivered to LIN network due to parity bit computation error
hazards.consequence: Slave nodes interpret wrong frame; incorrect actuator action
hazards.mitigation: REQ-LIN-001, REQ-LIN-002: protect_id verified by unit tests with golden vectors

hazards.description: Corrupted checksum accepted as valid frame
hazards.consequence: Invalid data processed by safety function
hazards.mitigation: REQ-LIN-003 through REQ-LIN-006: checksum algorithm verified by unit tests

hazards.description: Diagnostic frame (0x3C/0x3D) with enhanced checksum accepted
hazards.consequence: Incorrect diagnostic response; potential misconfiguration of slave node
hazards.mitigation: REQ-LIN-009: validate_frame enforces ClassicChecksum for diagnostic IDs

hazards.description: Frame data longer than 8 bytes sent on bus
hazards.consequence: Bus protocol violation; undefined slave node behaviour
Undefined behaviour on LIN network; potential bus conflict

hazards.description: NoResponse not propagated to application caller
hazards.consequence: Application assumes successful communication; stale state used
hazards.mitigation: REQ-LIN-014, REQ-LIN-021: NoResponse returned and maps to relay::Error::Timeout

hazards.description: E2E CRC computed incorrectly — corrupted payload accepted as intact
hazards.consequence: Safety function processes corrupted data; incorrect actuator command issued
hazards.mitigation: REQ-SAFETY-005, REQ-SAFETY-008: CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE verified by golden-vector unit test; CrcMismatch returned on detection

hazards.description: E2E sequence counter replay — replayed old frame accepted by Receiver
hazards.consequence: Stale data used by safety function; control output based on outdated sensor reading
hazards.mitigation: REQ-SAFETY-009: Receiver::unwrap returns SequenceGap when counter != last+1

hazards.description: LDF parser produces invalid schedule entry with frame ID > 0x3F
hazards.consequence: MasterNode sends header with illegal ID; bus protocol violation
hazards.mitigation: REQ-SEOOC-006: integrator must validate LDF-derived IDs before passing to MasterNode; MasterNode set_schedule enforces ID ≤ 0x3F (REQ-LIN-013)

hazards.description: SlaveNode set_response overwrites concurrent frame exchange in progress
hazards.consequence: Slave returns partially-updated data; corrupted frame broadcast to subscribers
hazards.mitigation: REQ-SLAVE-008: tokio::sync::Mutex serialises all publish/response operations; set_response atomically replaces entry

hazards.description: MasterNode schedule loop continues after context cancellation
hazards.consequence: Master sends unexpected frames; slave nodes in invalid state
hazards.mitigation: REQ-MASTER-004: MasterNode::run checks ctx.done() at top of each loop iteration

hazards.description: RELAY adapter from_message accepts message with wrong protocol, causing ID mis-mapping
hazards.consequence: Frame intended for different protocol interpreted as LIN frame; wrong actuator addressed
hazards.mitigation: REQ-ADAPT-002, REQ-SEC-002: from_message validates protocol field == Protocol::Lin (3) before constructing Frame
